title: Pairing, System Reasoning, Debugging, and Project Defense · Application Repair Board
summary: A realistic pairing, system reasoning, debugging, and project defense artifact contains one plausible targeting, evidence, integrity, access, privacy, process, or decision defect. Locate the first unsupported claim before revising it. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in multi-format technical defense packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
Reproduce the symptom, bound the last-known-good state, rank hypotheses, run discriminating checks, repair the cause, and retain regression evidence.
Elicit stakeholders, workload, quality scenarios, data, trust, failure, cost, operations, and evolution before proposing components.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Pairing Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The debug variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.

Inspect the Pairing Contract trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Pairing Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Pairing Contract, keep Pairing Contract connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Pairing Contract, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Pairing Contract, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Pairing Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Pairing Contract, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The debug variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Pairing Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: The strongest candidate should take control and finish alone.

stimulus.title: Pairing Contract case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A candidate dominates a pair task, guesses at a bug, draws fashionable architecture, and claims a team project without separating personal work. The debug variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The strongest candidate should take control and finish alone.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise. Artifact under review: multi-format technical defense packet.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Pairing Contract, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Pairing Contract.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Project Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a career-project-defense-1x51k2: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Project Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Project Defense into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Project Defense, keep Project Defense connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
career-project-defense-1x51k2:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
